steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "conservation efforts that—through community involvement and scientific ______ aim to bring adjutants back from near extinction." There is an opening em-dash after "that." The phrase "through community involvement and scientific [study]" is a parenthetical inserted between "that" and "aim." Em-dash pairs must be used in matched pairs to enclose a parenthetical — the blank needs a closing em-dash after the noun to close the interruption.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — study: leaves the opening em-dash unclosed, making the parenthetical run open indefinitely. ✗
  • Choice B — study,: a comma does not close an em-dash; mismatched punctuation. ✗
  • Choice C — study:: a colon does not close an em-dash; mismatched punctuation. ✗
  • Choice D: study—: correctly closes the opening em-dash, neatly enclosing the parenthetical phrase "through community involvement and scientific study." ✓
That matches Choice D — study—.
